text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025

Dr Helen Carter
Riverside Health Centre
22 Riverside Way, Kingston upon Thames, KT1 1AA

Dear Dr Helen Carter,

Amelia Thompson, DOB: 03-11-1991, Hospital No: H382914 NHS No. 9843216750
Flat 4, 7 Larch Close, Surbiton, KT6 5PD

Epilepsy Clinic

I am writing to confirm cancellation of the scheduled outpatient appointment for Amelia Thompson on 09 October 2025 at 10:30. She has advised that, due to her work commitments as an early-years childcare worker in a high-noise, multitasking setting where midday rest breaks are often interrupted, she is unable to attend at that time.

No urgent concerns were reported during her call. We have offered alternative early-morning appointment options to minimise disruption to her work schedule, and our bookings team will contact her within 5 working days. She may also rebook via the Patient Booking Service on 0300 123 4567 or by emailing epilepsy.bookings@nhs.net.

If her circumstances change or if you feel an earlier review is required, please let us know and we will prioritise a suitable slot.

Yours sincerely,

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter is solely about cancellation and rescheduling of an outpatient appointment. It mentions no clinical details about seizures, their presence, absence, or frequency. There are no numeric or qualitative statements regarding seizure occurrence. Therefore, there is no seizure frequency information to extract.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter confirming cancellation of an epilepsy clinic appointment

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
